comment: Heavy reliable path: statusLine usage + watch + wait + claude -c/--resume -p. No SendKeys.
comment_session: Set session_name if you start with: claude -n my-task

stop.comment: Soft-stop: at thresholds.stop_percent the watcher sends Ctrl+C to the interactive Claude window recorded by Start-Babysitter (that PID only), so the last percent of the 5h window is not burned mid-turn. The pinger keeps the cache warm until the reset; wait+continue at the hard limit is unchanged.

power.comment: SetThreadExecutionState(ES_SYSTEM_REQUIRED) while watcher/pinger run, so Windows idle auto-sleep cannot freeze them (the display may still turn off). Default false so install is non-invasive; enable for unattended/overnight runs on AC power.

continue.comment_mode: headless | interactive | both (headless first, then interactive window)
continue.retry_message: Continue where you left off after the usage limit reset. Re-read PROGRESS.md if present. Prefer the last agreed plan; do not re-litigate architecture unless blocked. Proceed autonomously.

keepalive.message: ACK only: context keep-alive. Do not run tools. Reply with OK.
messages: ACK only: context keep-alive. Do not run tools. Reply with OK.
Keepalive ping. No tools. Reply: OK.
Session warm-up. Do not change code. Reply with OK only.
Cache refresh. Ignore all tasks. Answer: OK.
Heartbeat. Do not use tools or edit files. Reply OK.
Idle maintain. No investigation. Reply with a single OK.
Prompt-cache touch. Stay idle. Respond: OK.
Standby check. Do not continue the project. Reply OK.
Lightweight ACK. Zero tool calls. Reply: OK.
Context hold. No PROGRESS updates. Reply OK.
keepalive.comment_message_pick: random_no_repeat | random | rotate; legacy 'message' is the fallback when 'messages' is empty
keepalive.comment: Ping-Session.ps1: headless --resume ping refreshes ~1h prompt cache while session is idle. Fires after a random idle interval between min and max (seconds precision), re-drawn after every ping. Costs a cache read (~10%) per ping.
